title: Stroller
abstract: A head support device (40) for supporting a user's head is disclosed. The device (40) comprises a first and second spaced-apart cushion member (42) coupled together by a neck receiving portion (44). The cushion members (42) comprise an outer covering of flexible material and are filled with a soft stuffing material, and a first strip of shape memory alloy (50, 52) is provided along at least a portion of the length of at least one of the cushion members or the neck-receiving portion.
claims:
Claims 1. A head support device for, in use, supporting a userâs head, the device comprising first and second spaced-apart cushion members coupled together by a neck receiving portion, the cushion members comprising an outer covering of flexible material and being filled with a soft stuffing material, wherein a first strip of shape memory alloy is provided along at least a portion of the length of at least one of the cushion members or the neck-receiving portion. 2. A head support according to claim 1 , wherein the first strip of shape memory alloy is provided along at least a portion of the length of the neck-receiving portion. 3. A head support according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the neck-receiving portion comprises an outer covering of flexible material, and/or wherein the outer covering of the cushion members and neck-receiving portion are integrally formed. 4. A head support according to claim 3, a head support according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a strip of shape memory alloy is provided along at least a portion of the length of one or both of the cushion members, and optionally wherein a strip of shape memory alloy is provided along at least a portion of the length of one or both of the cushion members beneath the outer covering of flexible material. 5. A head support according to any of the preceding claims, comprising a first strip of shape memory alloy provided along at least a portion of the length of the neck-receiving portion and a second strip of shape memory alloy provided along at least a portion of the length of each of the cushion members, the second strips of shape memory alloy extending at an angle to the first strip of shape memory alloy, and optionally wherein the second strips of shape memory alloy extend at an angle to the first strip of shape memory alloy, the angle substantially corresponding to the longest dimension of the respective cushion member. 6. A head support according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the cushion members are generally oval or kidney-like in shape and the neck-receiving portion extends, generally centrally between the inward-facing edges of the cushion members, and optionally wherein the cushion members are generally kidney-like in shape, with the neck-receiving portion extending generally centrally between the convex side edges of the two cushion members. 7. A head support according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the outer covering is formed of a natural fabric. 8. A head support according to any of the preceding claims, wherein at least the cushion members have a multi-layered covering comprising the outer covering, an inner layer, and a middle layer formed of a textile into which mPCM material has been integrated, and optionally wherein the inner layer is formed of a natural fabric. 9. A head support according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the stuffing in the cushion members comprises a breathable foam. 10. A head support according to claim 2, wherein the first strip of shape memory comprises two separate strips of shape memory alloy, each extending from one respective end of the neck-receiving portion and along a portion of its full length, terminating at a location beyond its centre, such that the two strips are aligned and the distal ends either meet or overlap, and optionally wherein the two overlapping ends of the first strip are loosely held in alignment by a coupling loop or the like, such that they are still longitudinally slidable relative to one another. 1 1 . A head support device according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising means for enabling it to be clipped or otherwise affixed to the back of a childâs car seat, a stroller seat or a wheelchair and/or to a harness thereof. 12. A wheel for a manually conveyable vehicle, comprising a circular outer rim and a concentric hub spaced apart therefrom, and a plurality of radial elements extending between the hub and the outer rim, wherein the radial elements are formed of an elastomeric material and each having integral first 

 and second portions, the first portion being nearest to the hub and having an elastic modulus greater than the second portion. 13. A wheel according to claim 12, wherein the radial elements comprise elongate spoke-like elements that extend radially from the hub to the inner wall of the outer rim, and wherein the thickness of each spoke varies along its length, becoming progressively thicker from the outer rim to the hub, and optionally wherein each spoke is curved and extends at an angle to the radius from the hub to the inner wall of then outer rim. 14. A wheel according to claim 12, wherein the radial elements form a web disposed between the outer rim and the hub. 15. A wheel according to claim 14, wherein the radial elements of the web comprise a polygonal shape and the web is coupled to the inner wall of the outer rim via a plurality of connecting portions, and the web is coupled to the outer wall of the hub via a plurality of elongate portions. 16. A wheel according to claim 12, 14 or 15, wherein each radial element comprises two sections extending between the inner wall of the outer rim and the outer wall of the hub: a first section comprising a bifurcated portion that extends from a distal end of a connecting portion toward the inner wall of the outer rim, and a second section comprising a pair of elongate portions, each extending substantially radially from a distal end of a respective arm of the bifurcated portion. 17. A wheel according to claim 16, wherein the elongate portions forming the second section extend from the distal ends of the bifurcated portion toward the outer wall of the hub. 18. A wheel according to claim 16 or 17, wherein an elastic modulus of the two arms of the bifurcated portion of the first section is less than an elastic modulus of the elongate portions forming the second section. 19. A wheel according to any of claims 16 to 18, wherein the thickness of the arms forming the bifurcated portion of the first section is less than the thickness of the elongate portions forming the second section. 20. A wheel according to claim 16, wherein each radial element further comprises a third section comprising a further bifurcated portion extending from a distal end of each of the two elongate portions forming the second section, the end of each arm of the further bifurcated portion of the third section being coupled with the end of an arm of a further bifurcated portion of the third section of an adjacent radial element to form an adjoining portion, and a fourth section comprising an elongate portion extending radially from each adjoining portion. 21. A wheel according to claim 20 wherein, the elongate portions forming the fourth section extend toward the outer wall of the hub. 22. A wheel according to any of claims 12 to 20, wherein the inner wall of the outer rim is formed or provided with a layer of elastomeric material from which the radial elements extend, and optionally wherein the radial elements are formed integrally with the layer of elastomeric material on the inner wall of the outer rim. 23. A wheel according to any of claims 12 to 22, wherein the outer rim is formed of an elastomeric material and the radial elements are formed integrally therewith of the same material. 24. A wheel according to any of claims 12 to 23, wherein the inner wall of the outer rim, between each radial element is formed with a plurality of ridges, each adjacent pair of ridges defining a respective groove therebetween, and optionally wherein each ridge comprises a tapered distal end and has a profiled outer wall at its proximal end that results in each groove having a generally tapered base; and/or wherein the maximum width of the grooves is between 1.5 and 3mm. 25. A wheel according to any of claims 12 to 24, wherein the radial elements are substantially equally spaced all the way around the inner wall of the outer rim, each beneficially with an equal number of ridges and grooves therebetween. 26. A wheel according to any of claims 12 to 25, further comprising a layer of elastomeric material disposed on an outer side of the outer rim, the layer providing a grip between the wheel and the surface over which the wheel is arranged to travel. 27. A load carrying element for a manually conveyable vehicle, the load carrying element comprising a load receiving surface and a pair of opposing edge panels, the load carrying surface comprising a head portion and a lower body portion, and being configured to, in use, carry an infant or young child such that their head is located within the head portion, each said edge panel comprising or having a perforated panel of flexible material and a releasable cover configured, in a first configuration, to cover said perforated panel and to be selectively released to uncover said perforated panel and allow air to flow therethrough. 28. A load carrying element according to claim 26, wherein the load carrying surface is provided with a multi-layered covering comprising an outer layer of natural fabric and an inner layer of natural fabric, and optionally wherein the covering of at least the head portion includes a middle layer formed of, or including, a textile into which mPCM material has been integrated. 29. A load carrying element according to claim 26 or claim 27, comprising a bassinet, comprising a substantially flat base and having a pair of opposing side walls and a pair of opposing end walls, the flat base comprising said load receiving surface having two portions along its length, namely a head portion near a first of the end walls and a lower body portion forming the remainder of the load receiving surface to the opposing end wall, and optionally wherein the load receiving surface is flat and the head and lower body portions have an integral outer cover, and further optionally comprising a mattress removably fitted within the base of the bassinet and defining the load receiving surface, the mattress optionally comprising a resiliently deformable panel of breathable foam material having an outer cover comprising at least an outer layer of natural fabric, the outer cover optionally comprising an outer layer and an inner lining layer of natural fabric, the outer cover further optionally comprising a middle layer, between the outer layer and the lining layer, the middle layer covering at least a generally central section of the head portion of the load receiving surface and being formed of a textile into which mPCM material has been integrated. 30. A load carrying element according to claim 29, wherein the perforated panels are provided in both of the end walls of the bassinet, and optionally wherein each perforated panel has a respective cover, comprising a panel of flexible material, stitched or otherwise affixed to the bassinet along a side edge of a respective perforated panel and including securing means at its free end, and wherein cooperative securing means are provided on the bassinet, such that the cover flap can be selectively releasably secured over the perforated panel when ventilation is not required, and further optionally wherein second cooperative securing means are provided at another location on the bassinet and positioned such that the cover flap can be pulled back from the perforated panel and releasably secured away therefrom. 31. A load carrying element according to claim 27 or claim 28, wherein an elongate temperature indicating strip is provided on the inner wall of the bassinet, at the head end thereof. 32. A load carrying element according to claim 27 or claim 31 , comprising a child receiving seat having a base, a seat back and a pair of opposing side panels at least partially spanning the side region of the seat between the base and the seat back and each comprising or including a said perforated panel, and optionally wherein each cover is stitched, or otherwise affixed, along the edge of the seat back adjacent a respective side panel and of the same, or substantially similar, shape and orientation to the respective side panel such that, in use, it can be secured over the side panel to prevent ventilation therethrough, or pulled back from the side panel, to completely expose the perforated panel, when ventilation is required, and optionally further comprising securing means provided at the free end of the cover to secure it over the perforated panel, as required, and cooperative securing means are provided on the rear of the seat back to secure the cover behind the seat, when the perforated panel is required to be exposed. 33. A load carrying element according to claim 32, wherein the seat back is provided with a multi-layered outer cover comprising an outer layer of natural fabric an inner lining of natural fabric and a middle layer of temperature 

 regulating material; and/or wherein a pad is provided over the seat back, which has convex side edges. 34. A manually conveyable person carrying device comprising a head support according to any of claims 1 to 1 1 , and/or a wheel according to any of claims 12 to 26, and/or a load carrying element according to any of claims 27 to 32, further comprising a hood comprising a retractable canopy attached (removably or otherwise) or attachable to the side edges of the person carrying device so as to extend over a portion of a person receiving opening thereof when in an extended configuration, the hood having a front edge and a rear edge between which the canopy is suspended, wherein a pocket is provided at or near the front edge, the pocket housing an extendible, flexible cover configured, in use, to extend over the person receiving opening of the person carrying device and comprising a layer of UV reflective and/or protective material configured to be removably secured over the said person receiving opening, and optionally wherein the layer of UV reflective/protective material includes a perforated panel, and optionally further comprising a releasable cover configured to be releasably secured over said perforated panel. 35. A manually conveyable person carrying device according to claim 34, wherein the layer of UV reflective/protective material is substantially light blocking; and/or wherein the hood comprises a front rib defining the front edge and rear rib defining the rear edge and at least two intermediate ribs along the length of the canopy, wherein the pocket containing the extendible cover is provided along or adjacent an intermediate rib nearest the front edge of the hood; and/or 30 wherein the extendible cover is removably attached within the pocket; and/or wherein a second pocket is provided at the rear edge or along or adjacent an intermediate rib nearest the rear edge, and wherein a rain cover is housed (removably or otherwise) within the pocket and shaped and configured to be selectively extended over the hood and releasably secured over the person receiving opening as required, and optionally wherein the rain cover is formed of waterproof clear PVC.
